focus: D1 implementation-spec-alignment - target packet vs marker sweep intent and 010 graph context

findingDetails.title: 007 packet does not document the marker-sweep purpose it is supposed to authorize
findingDetails.evidence: The target spec, plan, and tasks remain scaffold placeholders while the concrete marker implementation is create.sh appending SCAFFOLD_VALIDATION_COUNTS and SCAFFOLD_AI_PROTOCOL_MARKERS.
findingDetails.recommendation: Replace scaffold placeholders with marker-sweep-specific spec, plan, tasks, checklist, and acceptance evidence.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Reviewed target spec.md, plan.md, tasks.md, checklist.md, implementation-summary.md, decision-record.md, and create.sh marker emission lines; only appended marker comments are marker-specific.

claimAdjudication.claim: The 007 packet does not document the marker-sweep purpose it is supposed to authorize.
claimAdjudication.counterevidenceSought: Checked target implementation-summary.md, decision-record.md, graph metadata, parent 010 docs, and 003 implementation ledger.
claimAdjudication.alternativeExplanation: The packet may intentionally be a freshly scaffolded fixture, but the review target is the approved authority for this sweep and no target resource-map exists.
claimAdjudication.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade to P2 only if the workflow formally treats this 007 packet as a generated fixture rather than a spec packet that gates implementation or release-readiness decisions.

findingDetails.title: 007 packet metadata is disconnected from the 010 phase-parent graph
findingDetails.evidence: The target graph metadata has parent_id null and description parentChain empty, while the 010 parent graph children_ids omit 007 and the parent phase map lists only 001-003.
findingDetails.recommendation: Refresh 007 metadata with the correct parent chain and update 010 child/status surfaces so 007 is represented consistently.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Cross-checked target graph-metadata.json, target description.json, 010 graph-metadata.json, and 010 spec.md; all parent-link surfaces are stale or missing for 007.

claimAdjudication.claim: The 007 packet metadata is disconnected from the 010 phase-parent graph.
claimAdjudication.counterevidenceSought: Checked target and parent graph/description/spec metadata for alternate link naming or archived relationship.
claimAdjudication.alternativeExplanation: The packet may have been scaffolded after the 010 parent metadata was last generated, but the current review target depends on discoverability.
claimAdjudication.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade to P2 if the deep-review workflow intentionally permits orphan child packets and never relies on parent graph traversal for this target.

noveltyJustification: First iteration established two active alignment findings across target docs and graph metadata.
ruledOut: No P0 security or destructive behavior found in this alignment pass.
Do not treat the marker comments themselves as defects; they match create.sh emission.
Historical 006 review artifacts are context only and not artifact authority.

focus: D2 code-correctness - marker emission and validator comment-consumer assumptions

findingDetails.title: Scaffold marker comments are counted as authored validator evidence
findingDetails.evidence: create.sh appends comment-only REQ-*, **Given**, and AI-protocol marker text, while check-section-counts.sh and check-ai-protocols.sh grep full markdown without stripping HTML comments.
findingDetails.recommendation: Update semantic validators to ignore SCAFFOLD_* HTML comment blocks or classify them as scaffold-only sentinels rather than authored validation evidence.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Checked marker producer, target scaffold output, scoped validator rules, and scaffold/template tests; the comment-counting consumer path is isolated to section-count and AI-protocol semantic checks.

claimAdjudication.claim: Scaffold marker comments are counted as authored validator evidence.
claimAdjudication.counterevidenceSought: Checked marker append guards, validator grep consumers, target scaffold output, structural validators, and scoped tests for comment-stripping or scaffold-marker exceptions; none was found for section-count or AI-protocol semantic consumers.
claimAdjudication.alternativeExplanation: The marker comments may be intentional bootstrap sentinels to let fresh scaffold packets clear non-authoring count checks, but strict validation currently cannot distinguish that scaffold-only bypass from real authored content.
claimAdjudication.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade to P2 if the validator contract explicitly treats SCAFFOLD_* comments as non-release scaffold sentinels and strict/release-readiness validation rejects or ignores them when authoring evidence is required.

noveltyJustification: New code-correctness evidence shows marker comments are consumed as semantic validation evidence by two validator rules.
ruledOut: No P0 security, data-loss, or destructive runtime behavior found.
No normal-rerun idempotence defect in marker append guards.
No AI-protocol marker Level-gating defect for Level 3/3+ scaffolds.

focus: D3 template-rendering-correctness - scaffold marker placement, template-source contracts, inline gates, and snapshot coverage
noveltyJustification: New template-rendering evidence confirms scaffold markers are absent from manifest template sources and are appended only after inline-gate rendering and template-source normalization; no new defect beyond the existing validator-consumer F003 was found.
ruledOut: No template-source placement defect: generated spec.md and plan.md keep SPECKIT_TEMPLATE_SOURCE immediately after frontmatter while scaffold markers land near EOF.
No manifest-template contamination defect: scoped manifest templates do not contain SCAFFOLD_* marker strings.
No inline-gate rendering defect for marker comments: inline gates are stripped by the renderer and scaffold markers are appended later by create.sh.
Do not duplicate F003; marker corruption remains a validator-consumer issue rather than a rendering issue.
No P0 security, data-loss, or destructive runtime behavior found.

focus: D4 validator-coverage - default validation orchestration, semantic marker validators, and negative fixture coverage

findingDetails.title: Active validation path omits the semantic marker validators
findingDetails.evidence: validate.sh exits through the Node orchestrator before legacy shell semantic rules run, while the Node orchestrator does not implement SECTION_COUNTS or AI_PROTOCOLS even though the registry declares both as active error validators.
findingDetails.recommendation: Port SECTION_COUNTS and AI_PROTOCOLS behavior into the Node orchestrator or continue executing registered shell semantic validators after the Node path, then add negative SCAFFOLD_* HTML-comment fixtures.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Reviewed default dispatcher, Node orchestrator entries, validator registry, semantic shell validators, target marker output, scoped Vitest tests, and legacy shell tests; this is distinct from F003 because it is an orchestration/test-coverage blind spot rather than the direct grep counting behavior.

claimAdjudication.claim: The active validation path omits the semantic marker validators that should guard against comment-only scaffold evidence.
claimAdjudication.counterevidenceSought: Checked validate.sh fallback ordering, Node orchestrator entries, registry metadata, scoped Vitest tests, legacy shell validation tests, and target strict-validation output for any active SECTION_COUNTS or AI_PROTOCOLS execution path.
claimAdjudication.alternativeExplanation: The Node orchestrator may be intended as a newer replacement for the legacy shell rules, but then it still needs equivalent semantic rule coverage or the registry should not advertise those rules as active error validators.
claimAdjudication.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade only if the command contract intentionally excludes SECTION_COUNTS and AI_PROTOCOLS from default strict validation and release-readiness relies on a separate documented validation command that is covered by tests.

noveltyJustification: New validator-coverage evidence shows default strict validation bypasses the semantic shell rules that would need a SCAFFOLD_* comment regression test, so the gap is broader than F003's direct grep consumers.
ruledOut: No duplicate of F003: F003 is the comment-counting behavior in shell validators; F004 is the default orchestration and coverage path omitting those validators.
No new placeholder, template-source, template-header, or anchor finding; those validators do not count marker comments as semantic evidence.
No P0 security, data-loss, or destructive runtime behavior found.

focus: D5 cross-runtime-mirror-consistency - command, agent, mirror, and cli-copilot target-authority parity

findingDetails.title: cli-copilot deep-review authority guard is not applied consistently across auto and confirm workflows
findingDetails.evidence: Auto mode passes targetAuthority with `type` while buildCopilotPromptArg branches on `kind`; confirm mode bypasses buildCopilotPromptArg entirely for cli-copilot.
findingDetails.recommendation: Pass `{ kind: 'approved', specFolder }` from auto YAML, add missing-authority fallback, and route confirm cli-copilot through the same helper before allowing --allow-all-tools.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Reviewed auto and confirm YAML, executor-config helper, command entrypoint, prompt pack, canonical agent, and runtime mirrors; mirrors are clean, defect is isolated to cli-copilot YAML/helper wiring.

noveltyJustification: One new P1 was found in the final cross-runtime pass: cli-copilot target-authority wiring is inconsistent and can omit the authority preamble in auto/confirm executor paths.
edgeCases: Prompt-pack stale doctrine path is already recorded by packet 005 and was not duplicated as a new 007 finding.
Confirm-mode risk is not the executor used by this run, but it is the paired command surface and proves parity drift.
Helper implementation is present; the defect is YAML call shape and confirm-mode bypass.
